questions.question: The function $f(x) = x^2$ is shifted 3 units to the right. What is the new equation?

The ancient Egyptians built the Great Pyramid around $2580$ BC. If we model the height of the pyramid over time $t$ (in years) using the function $h(t) = -0.1t + 150$, what is the vertical shift of this function?
A horizontal reflection over the $y$-axis is applied to the function $f(x) = x^3$. What is the equation of the reflected function?

The Rosetta Stone was carved in $196$ BC. If the function $p(t) = 2t + 10$ models the number of people $p$ visiting the stone $t$ years after it was carved, what is the $y$-intercept of this function?
A vertical stretch by a factor of $2$ is applied to the function $f(x) = x^2$. What is the equation of the stretched function?

The Hanging Gardens of Babylon were one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World. If a vertical compression by a factor of $0.5$ is applied to the function $f(x) = x^3$, what is the equation of the compressed function?

The ancient Greeks used the function $d(t) = 2t$ to model the distance $d$ (in meters) a runner could cover $t$ minutes after the start of a race. What is the horizontal shift of the function $d(t) = 2(t - 5)$?

The Great Wall of China was built to protect the Chinese Empire from invaders. If a reflection over the $x$-axis is applied to the function $f(x) = -x^2$, what is the equation of the reflected function?

The function $f(x) = x^2 - 2x + 1$ models the path of a projectile launched from the ancient city of Pompeii. Describe the transformations applied to the parent function $g(x) = x^2$ to obtain $f(x)$, and graph both functions on the same coordinate plane.

Compare the graphs of $y = f(x) = x^3$ and $y = g(x) = -x^3 + 2$. Describe any vertical or horizontal shifts, stretches, compressions, or reflections that have been applied to the graph of $f(x)$ to obtain the graph of $g(x)$. Use complete sentences and provide at least two key points of comparison.

teacher_rules: Use historical context to make questions more engaging and relevant to students' interests.
Ensure that all multiple-choice options are plausible but only one is correct.
Allow students to use graphing calculators or software to visualize and explore function transformations.
